prompt:
Declaration (in prose format):

Build an email triage rule that runs every 15 minutes against urgent emails
in my inbox. For each email, classify it using gpt-5.5. If it's spam,
archive it. If it's a billing email, route to the finance team. If the
sender is a VIP and the tone is angry, send me an SMS notification.
Otherwise, leave the email alone in the inbox.

Output a flat bullet list manifest of every concrete element this declaration implies.
manifest:
• Triage rule scheduled to run every 15 minutes
• Triage rule targets the inbox folder
• Triage rule filters for emails marked/tagged as urgent before processing
• Each qualifying email is passed to GPT-5.5 for classification
• GPT-5.5 is used as the classification model (not any other model)
• Classification must produce at least the following categories: spam, billing, VIP-angry, other
• Spam classification action: archive the email
• Billing classification action: route email to the finance team
• VIP + angry tone classification action: send an SMS notification to the rule owner
• Other/unmatched classification action: leave the email in the inbox untouched
• VIP sender detection must be evaluated as a condition
• Tone analysis (angry vs. not angry) must be evaluated as a condition
• Both VIP status and angry tone must be true simultaneously to trigger SMS
• SMS notification recipient is the inbox owner (the person who defined the rule)
• The rule operates on each email individually (per-email iteration)
• Archiving removes the email from the inbox view without deleting it
• Routing to finance team implies the email is forwarded or assigned to a finance team inbox/address
• The classification step precedes all routing/action steps
• The rule runs continuously on a 15-minute interval (recurring, not one-time)
